name: Why Rome Fell — And Why It Still Matters

layers.name: Headline
layers.content: HOW DOES A CITY THAT CONQUERED THE MEDITERRANEAN LOSE ITS EMPIRE?

layers.name: Headline
layers.content: ROME’S POWER WASN’T JUST MILITARY MUSCLE,

layers.name: Subhead
layers.content: DROP A COMMENT AND FOLLOW FOR MORE!

full_script: How does a city that conquered the Mediterranean lose its empire? Rome didn’t just fall overnight— something deeper broke: Rome’s unity as a vast system. Rome’s power wasn’t just military muscle, it was the rules and unity that held it together. Now, historians ask: what really made empires rise—or break? Books like Why Nations Fail say: it’s the system’s rules—its. So—was Rome destroyed from outside, or did it fail from.
